citations.description: Ticket comment by Joe Oakhart: 'Pushed to Gerrit as change 819... Awaiting LLM-Review +1 and Verified +1 before submit.'

findings.criterion: Lands through Gerrit with LLM-Review +1 and Verified +1.
findings.detail: This criterion is untagged and therefore codebase-verifiable by default. The ticket status remains 'in_progress' and the sole comment explicitly states 'Awaiting LLM-Review +1 and Verified +1 before submit' — an acknowledgment that those votes have NOT yet been received, not an attestation of completion. There is no codebase evidence (e.g., a merged commit) that the change landed.
findings.remediation: Record a ticket comment once the change has received both LLM-Review +1 and Verified +1 votes and has been submitted/merged, including the Gerrit change URL (https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/819), the vote results, and the timestamp. Also tag this criterion [operator-attested] in the ticket if the intent is for Gerrit vote records to serve as the admissible evidence.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
